Ecosystem Layer 01

Smart DB.

The Foundation Layer

Intelligent, grid-aware power balancing. The Smart DB sits at the root of your property's electrical infrastructure, throttling charging speeds in real time to secure grid transformer safety.

125A
Max Input Draw
5ms
Load Throttling Response
6x
Parallel Charger Loops
Assess Your Building Load →
Intelligent Current Balancing

Scale EV bays without utility grid upgrades.

Building power grids are finite. Adding dozens of standalone EV chargers risks systemic main breaker dropouts when building coolers and lifts operate concurrently.

The Smart DB serves as the dynamic coordinator. By monitoring aggregate building consumption, it dials down current to active EV bays in microsecond intervals during peak hours, and accelerates charging speeds overnight when demand drops.

Integration Blueprint

How the Smart DB Coordinates Power

Installed in the main utility room, the Smart DB establishes a safe, modular bridge between raw municipal transformer grids and your charging nodes.

01

Transformer Protection Loop

Continuous feedback clamps max current draws below the safety rating of local utility feeds.

02

Ecosystem Telemetry

Pushes live load statements and diagnostic reports to the EBEE Dashboard via encrypted eSIM cellular modules.

03

Modular Expansion

Allows you to scale from 2 to 6 loop lines without expensive breaker rebuilds.

Deployments

Tested under real property loads.

Case Study: Residential

Greenwood residency load balance

Greenwood society operated under a strict 45kW municipal transformer cap. Installing standard private chargers meant risking total grid failure when more than six vehicles charged simultaneously.

Outcome: Deployed a single Smart DB, expanding slot capacities to 12. Spared Greenwood from a ₹4.5 Lakh transformer rebuild.

Case Study: Commercial

Nexus Mall peak load balancing

Nexus Mall basement parking needed to maintain 15 chargers. During weekend shopping peaks, elevators and cooling loops drew massive power, causing localized voltage fluctuations.

Outcome: Smart DB coordinates load parameters between air conditioning loops and EV lines. Voltage dropouts cut to zero.

Technical details

Smart DB System Specifications

Comprehensive electrical parameters engineered to meet Indian grid guidelines.

Power Parameters

Input Voltage 3-Phase 415V AC ±15%
Frequency 50 Hz
Max Input Draw 125A Max

Safety & Output

Loops Supported Up to 6 Simultaneous
Response Time 5 Milliseconds
Protection Class II SPD, RCCB

Casing & Sync

Ingress Rating IP65 / IK10 Weather
Connectivity 4G LTE eSIM, RJ45
Protocol OCPP 1.6J / 2.0.1

Establish a grid-safe foundation.

Speak with an installation engineer to assess your grid limits.
